mission.title: Install and Configure Strimzi Kafka Operator
mission.description: Strimzi Kafka Operator allows you to run Apache Kafka clusters on Kubernetes, simplifying the deployment and management of Kafka. It is ideal for users looking to leverage Kafka's capabilities in a cloud-native environment. Key trade-offs include the need for Kubernetes expertise and the complexity of managing Kafka configurations.

steps.title: Add the Strimzi Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Strimzi Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add strimzi https://strimzi.io/charts

helm repo update

```

> This is the official repository for Strimzi charts.
> Updates your local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install the Strimzi Kafka Operator
steps.description:
This command installs the Strimzi Kafka Operator in the 'kafka' namespace using a specific version.

```bash

kubectl create namespace kafka

helm install strimzi strimzi/strimzi-kafka-operator --namespace kafka --version 0.44.0

```

> Create a namespace for the Kafka operator.
> Installs the Strimzi Kafka Operator.

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the Strimzi Kafka Operator is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n kafka

```

> This command lists all pods in the 'kafka' namespace to verify the operator is running.

steps.title: Create a Kafka cluster
steps.description:
Deploy a Kafka cluster using a custom resource definition (CRD).

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://strimzi.io/examples/latest/kafka/kafka-persistent.yaml

```

> This YAML file contains the configuration for a Kafka cluster.

steps.title: Verify the Kafka cluster
steps.description:
Ensure that the Kafka cluster is up and running.

```bash

kubectl get kafka -n kafka

```

> This command checks the status of the Kafka cluster.

uninstall.title: Uninstall the Strimzi Kafka Operator
uninstall.description:
This will remove the Strimzi Kafka Operator and all associated resources. Be aware that this may lead to data loss if you have persistent volumes attached to your Kafka cluster.

```bash

helm uninstall strimzi --namespace kafka

kubectl delete namespace kafka

```

> Uninstalls the Strimzi Kafka Operator.
> Deletes the Kafka namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Strimzi Kafka Operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your Kafka data. This process includes draining the operator, upgrading, and verifying the installation.

```bash

kubectl drain <operator-pod-name> --ignore-daemonsets

helm upgrade strimzi strimzi/strimzi-kafka-operator --namespace kafka --version 0.44.0

kubectl get pods -n kafka

```

> Drains the operator pod to prepare for upgrade.
> Upgrades the Strimzi Kafka Operator to the specified version.
> Verifies that all pods are running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to Kafka broker'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Kafka broker may not be running or is misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Check the Kafka broker logs for errors using 'kubectl logs <broker-pod-name> -n kafka'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'No resources found in kafka namespace'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Kafka cluster may not have been created successfully.

**Fix:**
Verify the Kafka CRD was applied correctly and check for errors in the creation process.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Strimzi Kafka Operator not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Strimzi Kafka Operator may not be installed in the specified namespace.

**Fix:**
Ensure the operator is installed using 'helm list -n kafka'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Kafka cluster is not ready'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Kafka cluster may be in the process of starting up or has encountered an error.

**Fix:**
Check the status of the Kafka cluster using 'kubectl describe kafka <cluster-name> -n kafka'.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Kafka 3.4.0, improved metrics collection.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from 0.43, ensure your Kafka configurations are compatible with the new version.

resolution.summary: You should see the Kafka cluster status as 'Ready' when running 'kubectl get kafka -n kafka'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with sufficient resources.
